title: Correlation coefficient (r)
short: A number from −1 to +1 measuring the strength and direction of a linear relationship between two variables.
full:
The correlation coefficient r summarizes how well two variables follow a straight-line relationship. It ranges from −1 (perfect negative) through 0 (no linear relationship) to +1 (perfect positive).

Values near ±1 mean the points hug a line; values near 0 mean little linear association. Correlation does not imply causation, and r only captures linear patterns — a strong curved relationship can still give a small r. It is computed from the same sums used for the regression line.
